title: lithocarpus macilentus
intro: Lithocarpus macilentus, also known as the Pacific madrone tree, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Pacific coast of North America. The bark of Lithocarpus macilentus is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Lithocarpus macilentus have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, providing health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and protection against oxidative stress. Lithocarpus macilentus can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing, soothe skin irritations, and support respiratory health.
